summary: Completed comprehensive testing of MoonLight v1.8 AI-Native Trading OS AI Reasoning Layer. Tested 15+ endpoints with 147/147 Jest tests passing. Found 1 routing issue with batch endpoint and 1 performance issue with rate limiting test.

critical_bugs.issue: Route conflict - batch endpoint being interpreted as single signal endpoint with id='batch'
critical_bugs.impact: Batch reasoning functionality not accessible via API
critical_bugs.details: Returns {error: 'signal_not_found', id: 'batch'} instead of processing batch request

performance_issues.issue: Rate limiting test takes too long (>120s timeout)
performance_issues.impact: Cannot verify rate limiting functionality in reasonable time
performance_issues.details: 35 rapid requests to test rate limiting exceed test timeout

passed_tests: GET /api/ai-coach/status - Returns available:true, model:'gemini-2.5-flash', reasoning_enabled:true, strict_guard:false
POST /api/ai-coach/reason-signal/:id - Single signal reasoning working with valid verdict/confidence/reasoning
GET /api/ai-coach/reasoning-history - Returns items with AI fields populated
GET /api/ai-coach/daily-insights - Returns totals, top_symbols, top_strategies, regime_distribution, ai_summary
GET /api/ai-coach/regime-heatmap - Returns symbols x timeframes matrix with regime/ADX data
GET /api/ai-coach/strategy-leaderboard - Returns strategies sorted by live_signal_count desc
POST /api/ai-coach/tune-strategy (valid) - Returns strategy stats and AI advice
GET /api/live/signals - Returns 1000+ signals with proper pagination
POST /api/ai-coach/chat - Turkish chat working with proper reply/model response
GET /api/data/providers/health - Returns 5 providers with MOCK_LIVE connected
POST /api/data/providers/auto-select - AI validation working with confidence scores
Jest Test Suite - All 147/147 tests passing with AI_REASONING_AUTO_BATCH=false
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix route conflict for POST /api/ai-coach/reason-signal/batch endpoint - likely needs route ordering fix in NestJS controller to prioritize 'batch' route before ':id' route
